draft.description: Scaffold validation. Reserved directories must exist, but marketplace catalog files may be absent.

published.description: Publishable validation. Marketplace catalog files must exist and reference plugin payloads inside the same bucket.

marketplace_buckets.name: curated-marketplace
marketplace_buckets.purpose: Favored third-party plugins, wrappers, mirrors, or compatibility packs.

marketplace_buckets.name: flow-marketplace
marketplace_buckets.purpose: Operating packs for upstream and self-hosted services.

marketplace_buckets.name: vibe-marketplace
marketplace_buckets.purpose: Original local projects and workflows that do not rely on upstream plugin ownership.

shared.rule: If a human edits the reusable capability itself, it belongs in shared.

catalog.role: structured package metadata and generation instructions
catalog.rule: If a human edits inventory, ownership, targets, provenance, package membership, or generator behavior for a plugin package, it belongs in catalog/plugins.

example_mapping.meaning: The shared path contains the skill body; the catalog path decides which Claude and Codex outputs include it.

skills.description: Root skills was the previous active local skill inventory. After migration, local skill source belongs under shared/skills.

invariants: Marketplace buckets live at repo root.
Each marketplace bucket is a Claude and Codex marketplace root.
Shared component roots are source inputs, not installed marketplace roots.
Reusable skill source lives under shared/skills.
Generated output is not canonical source.
Root skills is not a canonical source directory after migration.
Root plugins is legacy or scratch until explicitly migrated.
No active repository structure or docs reference unsupported platforms.

platform_notes.codex_commands: Codex supports custom slash commands locally, but this contract does not assume a Codex plugin-distributed commands surface.
platform_notes.codex_hooks: Codex hook docs emphasize config-layer discovery. Existing plugin manifests show a hooks field, so generators must treat Codex hooks as a distinct target instead of copying Claude hook semantics.
